codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal implementation that correctly moves the settings gear from .form-header-row to a new .page-header flex container alongside the h1 title. All five expectedBehavior items are satisfied: the gear and h1 share a flex row, gear is right-aligned via justify-content:space-between, chain selector has the full form row, click handler still works (same element ID preserved), and all aria attributes (aria-label, aria-haspopup, aria-expanded) plus keyboard handling (Escape closes modal, focus returns to gear) are intact. Only one instance of id="settingsBtn" exists — no duplication. The diff touches only src/server.ts (26 additions, 9 deletions) — purely a layout move with CSS additions. All verification commands passed (typecheck, lint, 129 tests). Worker also performed interactive checks via Playwright confirming correct rendering and modal behavior.

issues.description: Redundant CSS rule: .page-header .settings-btn { flex-shrink: 0; } duplicates the flex-shrink: 0 already declared in the base .settings-btn rule at line 796. No functional impact — just unnecessary specificity.

summary: Feature passes review. The settings gear was correctly relocated from .form-header-row to a new .page-header flex container alongside the h1 title. Implementation is minimal and correct — only layout CSS and HTML structure changed, no JavaScript modifications needed since the element ID was preserved. One non-blocking issue: a redundant flex-shrink:0 declaration in the .page-header .settings-btn selector that duplicates the base .settings-btn rule.
